permitted: One-family dwellings
Two-family dwellings
Multi-family dwellings
Manufactured homes (no older than 25 years; min 20'x40' on individual lots outside a Manufactured Home Park)
Manufactured home parks (per Article 14-5)
Public parks and public recreation grounds
Schools, churches, day-care/nurseries, rest homes and cemeteries
Customary household pets
Temporary construction buildings (use permit; max 12 months)
Utility buildings and structures (fenced and landscaped)
Gardens (garden crops; rear of lot; max 10% of lot area)
Fences, walls, hedges (per height limits)
Accessory buildings and uses (private garage, greenhouses, storage, swimming pools, home occupation)

zones.notes: Min lot 6,000 sf single-family / 8,000 sf two-family (recorded single-family 6,000). Min width 60 ft single-family / 80 ft two-family (recorded 60). Rear setback 20 ft, reduced to 10 ft where an alley is present. Side setback 10 ft. Max height 2 stories not to exceed 40 ft (excluding basement). District enumerated in § 14-3-1.

permitted: Residential units (detached or common-walled/attached) per cluster and master planned development standards

zones.source_citation: Land Code Art. 14-3 § 14-3-1; Art. 14-16 (Cluster and Master Planned Development) § II.A
zones.notes: CD enumerated as a Zoning District in § 14-3-1. Standards from Art. 14-16. Min site area: None. Min lot size 5,000 sf for detached dwelling unit. Detached-unit setbacks: front 10, side 5, rear 10 ft. Max building height 40 ft. Max dwelling units = gross site area / minimum lot size of underlying zoning district (max_density_dua TBD - depends on underlying zone). Attached-unit clusters use 10 ft site-boundary setback, buildings separated min 10 ft, up to 8 units/building.
zones.fetch_route: curl SPA JS bundle revealed PDF links; downloaded 14-3 and 14-16 PDFs via curl; pdftotext.

permitted: Manufactured home subdivision
Manufactured homes (not more than one unit per lot in a subdivision)
Accessory buildings and structures
Recreational facilities incidental to the manufactured home subdivision

zones.source_citation: Land Code Art. 14-5 (MHP District - Manufactured Home Park), § 14-5-3
zones.notes: District enumerated in § 14-3-1. Min manufactured home park area 3 acres. Min lot size 5,000 sf. Max lot coverage 30% on exterior lot / 35% on interior lot (recorded 35 = max). Max height 20 ft. Side setback 6 ft interior lots / 12 ft exterior lot lines (recorded 6 = interior). Min 10% of gross site area reserved as common/open space.

permitted: Retail establishments (stores, antique shops, art galleries, restaurants, food carry-out, liquor stores)

Financial and personal service establishments and craft shops (banks, offices, barber/beauty, tailor, laundry/dry cleaning, key/gun shops, interior decorating, appliance/shoe repair, photographic, optical, news stands)
Private and public office buildings, clinics, clubs, museums, churches, public and private schools
Bowling alleys, theaters, pool halls, dance halls, night clubs, taverns, bars
Grocery and drug stores
Bakeries and confectionery shops
Motels, hotels and day care nurseries
Radio and television stations, printing and publishing, telephone exchange
Gasoline stations (retail sales only)
Manufactured home, trailer, RV, and automobile sales
Public/private garages, towing, and auto repair (excluding junk yards and salvage yards)
Public/private parks and recreation grounds
Accessory buildings and uses customarily incidental to above uses
prohibited: Outdoor storage of merchandise, materials, equipment, or junk

zones.notes: District enumerated in § 14-3-1. Max height 30 ft or 2 stories, whichever is greater. Front yard setback 20 ft (or, where a block straddles C and residential districts, equal to adjoining residential front yard but not exceeding 25 ft). Side and rear setbacks not specified in 14-6-3 = TBD. No min lot size/width stated. Parking per Section 14-8.

permitted: Assembly of electrical appliances, electronic instruments/devices, radios and phonographs (manufacture of small parts only)
Construction equipment (sales, service and rental)
Laboratories (experimental, photo/motion picture, research or testing)
Laundry and dry cleaning plants (wholesale and retail)
Manufacture, compounding, processing, packaging or fabrication of goods sold on premises (no objectionable dust, odor, noise, smoke, vibration, light/glare beyond zone)
Wholesale distributing houses and warehouses
Warehouses and storage, contractors offices, lumberyards, building materials, repair shops and storage yards (including outdoor storage if screened by 6-ft solid wall/fence)
Signs per Section 14-9
Accessory buildings and uses customarily incidental to above uses
prohibited: Warehousing and storage of explosive material

zones.notes: District enumerated in § 14-3-1 as IND-1 Light Industrial. Max height 40 ft or 3 stories. Min lot width 80 ft, min lot area 10,000 sf. Setbacks: front 20, side 10, rear 20 ft. Parking per Section 14-8-2.
